page: Microsoft Azure FX4ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It features 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration is highly dense, providing 84.0 GB of RAM, which translates to 21.0 GB per core. The CPU includes a substantial 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores reaching top-tier status. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance. However, multi-core scaling is a notable tradeoff; stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth is average for small cached blocks at 348.09 GB/sec, but drops to a weak 36.48 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. LLM inference performance is average for small 135M models but degrades to poor for larger 7B and 70B models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the FX4ms_v2 offers a high resource density of memory relative to its compute footprint, making it a specialized choice for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. It is well-suited for high-memory single-threaded tasks, database operations, caching layers like Redis (which achieved an average of 6.29 million operations per second in testing), and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure FX4ms_v2 is a memory-optimized, x86_64 virtual machine running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 vCPUs (2 physical cores) operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz, paired with an extensive 84.0 GB of RAM. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks highlight exceptional single-core and text-processing capabilities, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core scaling and large-block memory bandwidth represent clear tradeoffs, performing in the lower tiers. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads with low parallel processing demands, such as in-memory databases, Redis caching, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX4ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 4 vCPUs, and 84.0 GB of RAM. It excels in single-core tasks but exhibits weaker multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX4ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 84.0 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring high single-core clock speeds and dense RAM configurations for specialized, memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
84.0 GB of RAM providing a high 21.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512
No local storage bundled, but premium storage capable
Best suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and single-threaded tasks
